# Code Generation Guardrails

## Overview

This SDK is 63% auto-generated. Generated files are created by [OpenAPI Generator](https://openapi-generator.tech) from the [sdk-generator](https://github.com/openfga/sdk-generator) repository.

**Golden rule: DO NOT EDIT GENERATED FILES. They get wiped on regeneration.**

## Generated vs Hand-Written Files

| File Pattern | Generated? | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `model_*.go` | ✓ Yes | ~60 data model files (request/response structs) |
| `api_open_fga.go` | ✓ Yes | Low-level API client; deprecated in favor of `api_executor.go` |
| `streaming.go` | ✓ Yes | Streaming response handler (NDJSON) |
| `internal/constants/constants.go` | ✓ Yes | SDK constants (batch limits, retry config, etc.) |
| `docs/**` | ✓ Yes | ~90 API reference markdown files |
| `api_executor.go` | ✗ No | Hand-written; preferred pattern for new methods |
| `api_client.go` | ✗ No | HTTP client setup and lifecycle |
| `configuration.go` | ✗ No | SDK configuration validation |
| `errors.go` | ✗ No | Error types and retry logic |
| `utils.go` | ✗ No | Helper functions (pointer conversion, etc.) |
| `response.go` | ✗ No | Response wrappers |
| `client/*.go` | ✗ No | High-level SDK methods (Check, Expand, etc.) |
| `credentials/*.go` | ✗ No | Credential types and injection |
| `oauth2/*.go` | ✗ No | OAuth2 token flows and Bearer transport |
| `telemetry/*.go` | ✗ No | OpenTelemetry instrumentation |
| `internal/utils/retryutils/*.go` | ✗ No | Retry backoff and header parsing |

## How to Identify Generated Files

Generated files include this header:

```go
/**
 * Go SDK for OpenFGA
 *
 * API version: 1.x
 * Website: https://openfga.dev
 * Documentation: https://openfga.dev/docs
 * Support: https://openfga.dev/community
 * License: [Apache-2.0](https://github.com/openfga/go-sdk/blob/main/LICENSE)
 *
 * NOTE: This file was auto generated by OpenAPI Generator (https://openapi-generator.tech). DO NOT EDIT.
 */
```

Any file with this header is auto-generated.

## What to Do if You Need to Change a Generated File

1. **Do not edit it locally.** Local edits will be wiped on the next regeneration.
2. **Submit changes to sdk-generator.** Fork [github.com/openfga/sdk-generator](https://github.com/openfga/sdk-generator) and modify the OpenAPI Generator templates.
3. **Request a regeneration.** Once your changes are merged to sdk-generator, the FGA team will run regeneration and push updated files to go-sdk.

## Generated File Behavior in CI

- **golangci-lint excludes generated files** by design (see `.golangci.yaml`). Lint errors in generated code do not cause CI to fail.
- **Generated files are not tested individually.** Tests exercise the SDK interfaces that use generated types, not the types themselves.

## Preferred Patterns

### Adding New API Methods

**Use `api_executor.go`, NOT `api_open_fga.go`:**

- `api_open_fga.go` is generated and will be replaced. Avoid adding methods here.
- `api_executor.go` is hand-written and is the preferred layer for new functionality.
- Example: If you need to add a new request/response flow, add it to `api_executor.go` and use generated model types from `model_*.go`.

### Adding New Data Models

**Changes must go to sdk-generator:**

- All request/response structs are in `model_*.go` (generated).
- To add fields or new model types, update the OpenFGA API specification and OpenAPI Generator templates in sdk-generator.
- Do not manually edit `model_*.go` files.

### Adding Constants or Configuration

**Check where it belongs:**

- Retry limits, batch limits, user agent strings → `internal/constants/constants.go` (generated; submit to sdk-generator)
- SDK-specific constants not tied to the OpenFGA API spec → hand-written files (e.g., `credentials/credentials.go`)

## Regeneration Workflow

When a new version of the OpenFGA API is released:

1. The sdk-generator repo is updated with new OpenAPI spec and templates
2. OpenAPI Generator runs to produce new files
3. New `model_*.go`, `api_open_fga.go`, `streaming.go`, `constants.go`, and `docs/*` files are committed
4. Any hand-written files remain unchanged
5. Tests verify the SDK still works with new models and API methods

## Key Files to Never Edit

These files **will be completely overwritten** on regeneration:

- `/model_*.go` (all ~60 files)
- `/api_open_fga.go`
- `/streaming.go`
- `/internal/constants/constants.go`
- `/docs/*` (all ~90 files)

If you see a code review comment about a change to one of these files, it's an error. Request that the author revert and submit the change to sdk-generator instead.
